resumes.title: Product Manager, Billing & Operations
resumes.why: Pasted JD (Bangalore/Hyderabad; his #2 location). 75% on keywords (18/24) -- and this is a SIGNIFICANT STRETCH, on par with or beyond the Amex one: the 75% reflects transferable skills, not domain fit. Covered from real work: AI-first product management (a HEAVILY emphasized JD theme he genuinely fits -- ships GenAI, uses it across discovery/specs/docs, evaluates AI outputs), monetization & subscription understanding (LPP, renewals, ARPU), API-driven workflows (6-gateway payment integration = billing-adjacent), backend automation, SQL/BI + relational databases, technical specs, cross-functional (eng + business), strategic thinking, deal/sales support. The 6 keyword gaps ARE the core of the role and are genuine, non-wording-fixable domain gaps: billing/invoicing systems, automated provisioning, entitlement management, consumption metering, Salesforce CPQ, and SKU creation/NPI. His monetization experience is PRODUCT-level (pricing/renewals), NOT backend billing-systems/metering/provisioning infrastructure -- a different discipline. Honest read: apply only if targeting a pivot into billing/monetization-systems PM; the AI-first + payments-API + automation angle is a legitimate hook, but the billing-infrastructure/CPQ domain gap (plus the big-tech bar) is the real deciding factor. Note: Salesforce asks candidates to apply to <=3 roles per 12 months. Auto-fit to one full page (93.1%).

resumes.title: Product Manager - Retail & Insights (Retail Media Platform)
resumes.why: REFRESHED 2026-07-22 after Kshitiz confirmed real experience that legitimately closed 4 gaps -- rescored 80% -> 84% (21/25), no fabrication. NEWLY VERIFIED AND ADDED: (1) CONTENT MANAGEMENT -- built/deployed a grade- and course-wise content catalogue (surfaced by learner choice + teacher recommendation) and automated content generation with AI prompts, dissolving manual content-team effort; this satisfies the ESSENTIAL OR-list 'strong understanding of one or more of Ad Servers / DSPs / SSPs / CDPs / DMPs / Digital CMS / ERP' via CMS, which was previously a hard gap. (2) ATTRIBUTION -- centralized CRM lead-source management with inbound leads auto-tagged via UTM parameters and tracking cookies, outbound uploaded/entered. (3) AUDIENCE SEGMENTATION & TARGETING -- personalised course plans by learner grade, professional segments by industry + seniority, and keyword targeting on highest-volume search terms. (4) CAMPAIGN PERFORMANCE -- partnered with Marketing/Sales optimising lead flow, lead acceptance and best-performing sources. Also expanded the third-party partner list (Daily.co, 100ms, Convin, Servetel on top of payments/Gupshup/Exotel). STILL GENUINELY NOT CLAIMED (the 4 remaining gaps): auction dynamics/bidding/pacing, ad servers & DSPs/SSPs, Retail Media/AdTech domain, and microservices architecture (his answer described onboarding third-party SERVICES, which is not internal microservices). OPEN/UNUSED: he also described an in-house CDP-like behavioural-data unification + ops engine (3,500+ teacher scheduling, payroll, subscription billing) -- deliberately NOT put on the resume until he confirms which parts he personally built and whether 3,500+ is accurate (logged in experience_bank Open Questions). Honest read: now a solid fit on the role's actual craft (API-first integrations, partner lifecycle, reusable capabilities, CMS/catalogue, attribution, targeting); the remaining gap is AdTech auction mechanics + Retail Media domain, which is learnable but will be the screen. ORIGINAL SCORING NOTE: 80% on keywords (16/20). GENUINELY STRONG on the role's day-to-day craft, which is API-first platform integrations: he really does own third-party integration lifecycles end-to-end (6 payment gateways via APIs incl. status verification/webhooks, messaging via Gupshup/Venera Connect, telephony via Exotel/Tata Tele) as the primary partner point of contact, and has built REUSABLE configurable capabilities rather than point solutions (shared payments capability, omnichannel support service reused across app/LMS/CRM). Also covered: product requirements/user stories/acceptance criteria/integration specs, API contracts & data mappings, adoption + reliability + data-quality metrics, business+technical stakeholder management, cross-functional agile, roadmap & prioritisation of large ambiguous initiatives. Essentials met: 5+ yrs PM, and the degree OR-list (Business/Marketing/Eng/CS) is satisfied by his BBA (Business). THE REAL GAP is the DOMAIN, and 3 of the 4 keyword misses are it: digital-advertising concepts (bidding, pacing, auction dynamics, targeting, attribution, campaign delivery) -- an ESSENTIAL he entirely lacks -- plus the essential 'strong understanding of one or more of Ad Servers / DSPs / SSPs / CDPs / DMPs / CMS / ERP' (OR-list; he satisfies NONE of the alternatives), and Retail Media/AdTech domain (desirable). 4th miss: microservices (desirable). Honest read: BETTER fit than the Salesforce-billing or Amex stretches because the integration/partner/reusable-platform work is the same KIND of work he actually does -- just a different industry. But the AdTech domain is an essential he'd have to learn, so expect that to be the screen. Worth applying if open to a Retail Media/AdTech pivot. Auto-fit to one full page (94.8%).
